raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, gap, and contribution framing around typed inter-agent communication.","R1.2":"The request implies checking whether the prior-work setup, limitation claim, proposed method, and empirical conclusion form a coherent argument chain.","R1.5":"Draft improvement reasonably includes section- and paragraph-block ordering to sharpen the narrative arc from literature to gap to contribution.","R1.6":"Because the delegation is phrased generally as improving a draft, sentence-level clarity, flow, concision, and academic register are load-bearing.","RX.1":"Any improvement should disclose uncertainty where the draft lacks enough evidence to substantiate empirical or novelty claims.","RX.2":"The draft contains broad claims such as firstness and generalization, so avoiding overclaiming is an active cross-cutting responsibility.","RX.3":"The agent must remain within draft-improvement responsibilities and not perform unrelated research, experimentation, or artifact execution.","RX.4":"The projection should account for possible downstream harm from misleading scholarly claims, inflated performance statements, or unsupported generalization.","RX.5":"Improvements should preserve traceability to the provided draft and distinguish artifact-grounded changes from assumptions."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, gap, and contribution framing around typed inter-agent communication.
rationale.R1.2: The request implies checking whether the prior-work setup, limitation claim, proposed method, and empirical conclusion form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.5: Draft improvement reasonably includes section- and paragraph-block ordering to sharpen the narrative arc from literature to gap to contribution.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation is phrased generally as improving a draft, sentence-level clarity, flow, concision, and academic register are load-bearing.
rationale.RX.1: Any improvement should disclose uncertainty where the draft lacks enough evidence to substantiate empirical or novelty claims.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains broad claims such as firstness and generalization, so avoiding overclaiming is an active cross-cutting responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within draft-improvement responsibilities and not perform unrelated research, experimentation, or artifact execution.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for possible downstream harm from misleading scholarly claims, inflated performance statements, or unsupported generalization.
rationale.RX.5: Improvements should preserve traceability to the provided draft and distinguish artifact-grounded changes from assumptions.
